_meta.description: Cached LLM (Grok-4.3) enrichment for the targeted hybrid pass. Adjudications over agency/ag_build/grok_candidates.json. Committed so warehouse refreshes stay deterministic (INTEGRATION_BRIEF s7). Regenerate ONLY if the underlying extracted JSON changes.

adjudication_rules.questioned_cost_usd: Only the audit's OWN identified misspent/unsupported/improper/unrecovered dollars tied to a finding. Excludes: national stats, program funding levels, penalty schedules, restitution already recovered by others, contextual figures.
adjudication_rules.agency_bridge: Token/word-boundary match to checkbook agency_summary.agency; subprogram audits map to parent dept; non-state entities (regional COGs) marked no_checkbook_equivalent.
adjudication_rules.finding_gap: clean = report genuinely has no adverse findings; chaptered = findings exist under Chapter/Conclusion structure not the modern 'Finding N:' template.

agency_overrides.note: Title-page concatenated two entities ('...and the Arizona Veterans' Service Advisory Commission'); primary audited agency is DVS.
No agency-lead keyword on title page; recovered from 'Report 23-109' next line.
Truncated 'Arizona Families F.I.R.S.T.' program suffix; parent agency is DCS.
Parser joined topic 'Commercial Real Estate' onto agency name, causing a spurious REAL ESTATE DEPARTMENT bridge. True agency is ABOR; audit is about ABOR-held commercial real estate.

ASU/NAU/UA roll up to Board of Regents.
Arizona Power Authority — verify checkbook presence.
AHCCCS may render differently in checkbook; verify against agency list.
Racing Commission consolidated under Dept of Gaming.

questioned_costs.basis: Department staff identified $102,602 misspent program monies Aug 2015-Jan 2016 (Table 2); only ~$14,500 recovered as of Jan 2016.
~$167,000 spent on services for a client with private insurance, file lacked documentation of comparable-benefit search.
questioned_costs.note: Single illustrative case, not a total.

questioned_costs.basis: ~$19.7M reduction in misspent federal SNAP monies from payment-processing errors.
$950,104 in contract spending across 309 contracts lacked procurement-type documentation.
$1,500 investigative cost reimbursement lacked supporting documentation.
Unreconciled ITA items totaling ~$992,400 absolute dollar value; documentation could not be located for a sample.
CMS-projected ~$1.77B (16%) of AHCCCS payments FY2020 potentially improper due to eligibility-determination noncompliance.
questioned_costs.note: Projection, not confirmed loss; flag as estimate.

questioned_costs.basis: At least $330,000 administrative/technical costs allocated to federal programs FY2020-2022 may be unallowable.
$382,408 fraud across 177 customer accounts; $216,412 recovered, $165,996 unrecovered as of audit.

ACA lacked documentation verifying business-reported job-creation/capital targets before approving nearly $11M in incentives (Quality Jobs tax credits + A1 grants). Separately, $2.4M spent on private CEO Forums flagged as questionable marketing spend. (v2 sweep refined this up from the v1 $7.5M Quality-Jobs-only figure.)
$8.1M in grantee expenses approved/paid that were unsupported or unallowable.
$2M fraudulent wire transfer from State Housing Trust Fund (2023); insurance later covered loss.
State may owe ~$231.1M to federal govt if unreconciled cash consisted of recovered overpayments/fraudulent federal UI monies.
~$3,000 spent from funds prohibited for employee-recognition purposes, lacked public-purpose documentation.
$2.4M in UI overpayments to 925 ineligible claimants from un-updated IT system; waived, not recovered.
ABOR/UA did not adhere to master lease requirements, resulting in the inappropriate use of $3.9M and inadequate oversight of master lease agreements. (v2 sweep)

DVS could not provide completed approval forms for a $221K software-support payment and an $81K temp-staffing payment (~$302K unsupported), plus $45K uncollected from Arizona American Legion contract. (v2 sweep)
Board of Nursing accounting error (FY2018) led to ~$348K owed to the State General Fund as of July 2021. (v2 sweep)

Office of Tourism: $15,076 journalist-tour expenses did not comply with SAAM nonemployee-travel/receipt requirements; separately exceeded $100K delegated procurement authority on ~$500K in services (procurement noncompliance, not recorded as QC). (v2 sweep)
Dept of Education School Safety Program reimbursed 1 school $43,200 across 6 requests for a counselor without receiving supporting expenditure reports. (v2 sweep)
DCS reported ~$699K still owed since 2010 from participants who did not fulfill obligations; collected only ~$82K Feb-Jul 2017. (v2 sweep)

finding_gap_adjudications.note: Chapter 1 + recommendations; findings under chapter headers.
Board complaint-handling + public-info findings under 'The Board should' recs.
MAG RTA compliance review; multi-chapter performance review, largely on-track.
Pima RTA compliance review; performance mostly met targets.
DCS AFF contract controls largely established.
ADCRR capital funding 2-4% of requests; ITA reconciliation gaps.
Board of Barbers conflict-of-interest statutory noncompliance.
WIFA 'has established processes' - clean sunset review.
State Land Dept agricultural rental-rate appraisal failures.
School Facilities Oversight Board; implements all but 1 rec.
AG Anti-racketeering fund biennial review; all 3 prior recs implemented - clean.
